meta_title: Roofing in Emerald Hills, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Emerald Hills, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, A+ BBB. Free estimates. From $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing Experts Protecting Emerald Hills Homes From the Ground Up
intro:
Emerald Hills is one of Broward County's most sought-after residential communities, and for good reason. Tucked between Hollywood, Davie, and Cooper City, this established neighborhood offers tree-lined streets, beautifully maintained homes, and the prestige of Emerald Hills Country Club right in the backyard. With average home values hovering around $550,000, protecting your property investment starts at the top — and that means having a roof that can stand up to South Florida's relentless weather cycle. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've made it our mission to be the roofing company that Emerald Hills homeowners trust season after season.

South Florida's climate is unlike anywhere else in the country. The combination of intense UV exposure, year-round humidity, and the ever-present threat of tropical storms and hurricanes means that residential roofing here takes a beating that most materials simply aren't designed to handle long-term. In Emerald Hills specifically, the 33021 ZIP code falls within a 170 mph wind zone under Florida Building Code, meaning every new roof installation must meet stringent hurricane-rated standards — even though the area sits outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ). That distinction matters, and it shapes every product we specify and every nail pattern our crews follow.

Roughly 40% of homes in the Emerald Hills area were built before 1994, predating the sweeping code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. That means a significant number of homeowners in this community are living under roofing systems that were never designed to today's hurricane-resistance standards. Whether your home features the classic barrel tile look that complements the country club aesthetic or the architectural shingle profile common throughout the neighborhood's mid-century builds, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has the expertise, the products, and the licensed crews to deliver a complete roof replacement or targeted repair that protects your family and your investment for decades to come.

section_why.h2: Why Emerald Hills Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you're making a significant investment in a $550,000 home, you don't leave the roofing job to a fly-by-night crew chasing storm work. Emerald Hills residents deserve a contractor with deep local roots, verifiable credentials, and a track record that speaks for itself. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings 60+ years of combined installation experience to every project in Broward County, and we've completed roofing work on more than 500 South Florida homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau isn't an accident — it's the result of consistently delivering on our promises.

What truly sets us apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We don't subcontract your roof to the lowest bidder. Every crew member who shows up at your Emerald Hills home is a trained, background-checked Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who understands Florida's building code requirements, manufacturer installation specifications, and the quality expectations that come with working in an upper-middle-income community like this one. That in-house commitment means tighter quality control, cleaner job sites, and direct accountability — no passing the buck to a subcontractor when a question arises.

We also understand that Emerald Hills is a neighborhood with character. Homes here range from mid-century ranch-styles to more contemporary builds, and the roofing choices that complement each vary considerably. Our team takes the time to evaluate not just the structural needs of your roof, but the aesthetic profile of your home and what will add the most curb appeal and resale value. From the initial inspection to the final permit sign-off with the City of Hollywood Building Division, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step of the process so you don't have to.

And because we know that even well-positioned homeowners don't always want to pay for a major home improvement out of pocket all at once, we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available. Protecting your Emerald Hills home shouldn't require liquidating savings — we make it accessible without sacrificing quality.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Demands a Serious Roofing Solution in Emerald Hills
section_history.body:
The storm record in and around Emerald Hills is a sobering reminder of why hurricane-rated roofing isn't optional in South Florida — it's essential. Since 1990, this area of Broward County has been impacted by nine named storms, with three particularly significant events shaping how homeowners and contractors think about roofing today. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a generational wake-up call for all of South Florida, exposing catastrophic weaknesses in pre-existing roofing systems and prompting the building code overhaul that followed two years later. Many of the homes that survived Andrew in this part of Broward County still carry roofs installed in the aftermath — roofs that are now approaching or exceeding their design lifespan.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 moved through Broward County as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, and its wide pressure gradient meant that even areas not directly in the eye wall experienced severe wind and rain intrusion damage. Homes throughout the Emerald Hills community saw shingle blow-off, flashing failures, and underlayment exposure that led to extensive interior water damage. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, a massive storm that tracked directly up the Florida peninsula and reminded even well-maintained properties that age and exposure take a toll on roofing integrity over time.

This storm history underscores a critical truth for any homeowner in Emerald Hills: an aging roof isn't just a cosmetic issue. It's an active liability. A roof that hasn't been updated since before 2005 has already survived multiple major storm events and may be one severe weather season away from a catastrophic failure. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's roofing specialists conduct thorough inspections that evaluate decking integrity, fastener patterns, underlayment condition, and flashing seals — the full picture of your roof's storm-readiness — so you know exactly what you're working with before the next season arrives.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Emerald Hills and Broward County
section_code.body:
Navigating Florida's building code requirements for roofing can feel overwhelming, but for Emerald Hills homeowners, understanding the basics helps you make smarter decisions and avoid costly mistakes. The Emerald Hills area falls within Broward County's jurisdiction but routes its building permits through the City of Hollywood Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code (FBC) in full. While Emerald Hills is not designated as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a distinction that applies primarily to Miami-Dade County and parts of coastal Broward — the area still operates under the FBC's strict wind load requirements for Broward County, including a design wind speed of 170 mph.

What this means in practical terms is that every roofing product installed on a home in Emerald Hills must carry the appropriate Florida Product Approval (NOA or FL Number) certifying its performance at or above the area's wind rating. Roofing underlayments, fastener patterns, hip and ridge cap installations, and deck attachment methods are all governed by code and subject to inspection. Non-compliant installations can result in failed inspections, forced re-roofing, and — critically — denied insurance claims when you need them most. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's project managers are intimately familiar with City of Hollywood Building Division permitting requirements and handle the entire permit process on your behalf.

For homes built before 1994, the re-roofing process often triggers additional code compliance requirements related to the structural deck and attachment. Our team conducts a full code compliance assessment before any project begins, ensuring there are no surprises mid-project. We also ensure that all roof systems we install meet or exceed the FBC's Secondary Water Barrier (SWB) requirements and the Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) standards where applicable — giving Emerald Hills homeowners the most code-compliant, insurance-friendly roof installation possible.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Built for Emerald Hills
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier roofing product lines that are ideally matched to the architectural styles, climate demands, and aesthetic standards of the Emerald Hills community: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both brands carry the approvals necessary for installation in Broward County's 170 mph wind zone, and both are engineered to deliver exceptional longevity in South Florida's high-UV, high-humidity environment.

The CertainTeed Landmark Series is one of the most recognized and trusted architectural shingle lines in the industry. Unlike traditional three-tab shingles that were common on pre-1994 Emerald Hills builds, Landmark Series shingles feature a dual-layer laminate construction that creates a dimensional, slate-like appearance while dramatically improving wind resistance and impact durability. CertainTeed's Landmark shingles are rated for wind resistance up to 130 mph with enhanced installation techniques, and they carry a Lifetime Limited Warranty that protects your investment for the life of the home. For Emerald Hills homes with more traditional or transitional architectural profiles, these shingles deliver an upscale look without the weight load of tile.

For homes in Emerald Hills that carry or aspire to the barrel tile or flat tile aesthetic — particularly popular in the country club-adjacent properties near Emerald Hills Country Club — West Lake's concrete and clay tile systems offer unmatched curb appeal and hurricane-grade durability. West Lake tiles are manufactured to Florida's most demanding standards, with products tested and approved for Broward County's wind zone requirements. Concrete tile offers exceptional longevity (50+ years with proper maintenance) and thermal mass benefits that can reduce cooling loads. Clay tile carries the warmth and old-world character that complements Mediterranean and Spanish Colonial architecture beautifully. Both systems, when properly installed with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews using approved underlayments and fastening systems, represent the gold standard of residential roofing in South Florida.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Emerald Hills — And How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
For a home in Emerald Hills valued around $550,000, a full roof replacement is one of the most impactful investments you can make — both in terms of protecting that value and potentially enhancing it. The cost of a new roof in Broward County varies based on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, the condition of the existing deck, and whether any structural repairs are needed during the process. Generally speaking, Emerald Hills homeowners should budget for architectural shingle replacements in the range of $15,000 to $30,000 for a typical single-family home, while premium concrete or clay tile systems may range from $25,000 to $55,000 or more depending on scope and complexity.

Those numbers can feel significant, but context matters. A properly installed hurricane-rated roof on a $550,000 home isn't just a maintenance expense — it's a value-protecting, insurance-influencing, long-term financial asset. Roofing is consistently cited by real estate professionals as one of the top return-on-investment home improvements, particularly in South Florida's competitive market. Buyers purchasing in Emerald Hills actively look for updated roofs as a condition of sale, and many lenders and insurance carriers require it. The cost of not replacing an aging roof — in storm damage claims, insurance non-renewals, or deferred sale value — often far exceeds the cost of proactive replacement.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes the financial side manageable with flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified homeowners. We work with financing partners who understand the unique needs of South Florida homeowners and structure payment plans that fit real-life budgets. Whether you prefer to finance the full project over time or leverage an insurance claim to cover a significant portion of the cost, our team will walk you through every option so you can make the decision that makes the most sense for your household.

section_insurance.h2: Roofing Upgrades in Emerald Hills Can Unlock Real Insurance Savings
section_insurance.body:
One of the most underappreciated financial benefits of installing a new hurricane-rated roof in Emerald Hills is the impact it can have on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been under enormous pressure in recent years, with carriers tightening underwriting guidelines, raising rates, and in some cases non-renewing policies on homes with aging roofs. In Broward County, where wind exposure is a material risk factor, the age and condition of your roof is one of the single most influential variables in determining your annual insurance premium.

A new roof installation triggers what's known as a Wind Mitigation Inspection — a formal assessment conducted by a licensed inspector that evaluates your roof's ability to resist hurricane-force winds. When your roof features qualifying characteristics — including a hip roof shape, secondary water barriers, and products installed with approved fastening patterns — you may receive significant wind mitigation credits from your insurer. These credits can translate to annual premium reductions of 20% to 45% for Emerald Hills homeowners, depending on your carrier and coverage structure. On a policy for a $550,000 home, that kind of reduction adds up quickly and can meaningfully offset the long-term cost of your roofing investment.

Common carriers serving Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance and various private market insurers operating in the South Florida market — all recognize wind mitigation credits based on the Florida Uniform Mitigation Verification Inspection form. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs roofing systems specifically designed to maximize these credit opportunities, and our team can walk you through what to expect from the post-installation inspection process. We've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners not only get a better roof, but get a better rate on the insurance that protects it.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Emerald Hills home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on several factors: the age of your roof, the extent of any visible damage, and the condition of your roofing deck and underlayment. Homes in Emerald Hills built before 1994 — which represent about 40% of the local housing stock — may have roofing systems approaching or past their design lifespan. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free roof inspections where our licensed professionals assess all of these factors and provide an honest recommendation. In many cases, targeted repairs can extend a roof's life; in others, replacement is the more cost-effective and code-compliant solution.

faqs.q: What does a new roof cost for a typical Emerald Hills home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a standard single-family home in Emerald Hills, architectural shingle replacements typically range from $15,000 to $30,000, while concrete or clay tile systems range from $25,000 to $55,000+, depending on roof size, pitch, and scope. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our team will provide a detailed, transparent quote so you understand exactly what you're investing in before any work begins.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a roof replacement in Emerald Hills, and who handles them?
faqs.a: Roof replacements in Emerald Hills require a permit through the City of Hollywood Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code for this area of Broward County. All roofing products must carry valid Florida Product Approvals appropriate for the area's 170 mph wind zone design speed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to inspection scheduling and final sign-off — so you don't have to navigate the bureaucracy yourself.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement typically take for a home in Emerald Hills?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on single-family homes in Emerald Hills are completed within one to three days once materials are staged and permits are in hand. Larger homes with complex roof lines, tile systems, or structural deck repairs may take longer. The permitting process with the City of Hollywood Building Division typically adds one to two weeks to the overall project timeline. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides a detailed project schedule upfront, and our in-house crews work efficiently to minimize disruption to your household.

faqs.q: Can a new roof lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Emerald Hills?
faqs.a: Yes — often significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof qualifies your home for a Wind Mitigation Inspection, which can result in substantial premium credits from your insurance carrier. Broward County homeowners with qualifying roof characteristics — including hip roof geometry, secondary water barriers, and approved fastening systems — can see annual premium reductions of 20% to 45%. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs roofing systems designed to maximize these credits, and we help guide homeowners through the post-installation inspection process to ensure they capture every available savings opportunity.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Emerald Hills Home With a Roof Built to Last?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free roof inspection and no-obligation estimate — because in Emerald Hills, the best time to upgrade your roof is before the next storm season begins.
